title: RAG eval-set Q-A row
description: Schema for one row in golden.jsonl, calibration.jsonl, held-out.jsonl, or adversarial.jsonl. Produced by the building-rag-eval-set skill. Consumed by evaluating-rag-retrieval, auditing-chunking-strategy, auditing-embedding-drift, and any downstream RAG evaluation harness.

id.description: Stable ID for the row; never re-used even if the row is deleted.

answer.description: Ground-truth answer text. For absent-topic rows, this is the expected refusal or 'not in corpus' response.

kind.description: Question type. factoid = single-fact lookup; multi-hop = requires combining 2+ facts; synthesis = requires summarization across multiple spans; absent-topic = answer is not in the corpus.

difficulty.description: Difficulty band assigned by the reviewer.

split.description: Which split this row belongs to. held-out rows MUST NOT be viewed during development.

source_doc_id.description: Document ID of the primary source. Null only for absent-topic rows.
If different from the row-level source_doc_id (multi-hop / synthesis case).

source_spans.description: Character-offset spans inside the source documents that contain the answer evidence. Empty array only for absent-topic rows.

reviewed_by.description: Reviewer identifier; must match one of the reviewers list passed to the skill.

ground_truth_verified.description: True only when the reviewer confirmed the answer text matches the source span. Rows with false MUST NOT enter the golden set.

ambiguity_flag.description: True if the query is ambiguous and multiple defensible answers exist. Ambiguous rows may enter calibration but NOT held-out test.

drafted_by.description: How the candidate Q-A was drafted: 'human', or 'llm:<model_id@revision>'.
